findings.summary: 5 infrastructure modules use bare logging.getLogger() instead of the project-standard get_logger() from logging_utils
evidence: 90+ sibling modules consistently use `logger = get_logger(__name__)` from infrastructure.core.logging_utils
findings.suggestion: Replace `import logging` / `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` with `from infrastructure.core.logging_utils import get_logger` / `logger = get_logger(__name__)` in each of the 5 files. The bare stdlib logger bypasses the project's structured formatter, emoji support, and test-environment propagation setup.

findings.summary: generate_multi_project_report in reporting/__init__.py catches all exceptions and re-raises without wrapping in a typed TemplateError, losing context at a public boundary
evidence: Lines 108-110: `except Exception as e: logger.error(f'Multi-project reporting failed: {e}'); raise` — logs a bare string from `e` and re-raises the original untyped exception, whereas the rest of the infrastructure raises typed TemplateError subclasses at module boundaries
The surrounding pipeline infrastructure (pipeline.py, pipeline_reporter.py) consistently wraps exceptions in typed errors with structured context dicts
findings.suggestion: Wrap the re-raise: `raise BuildError('Multi-project reporting failed', context={'error': str(e)}) from e`. This preserves exception chaining and aligns with the typed error contract documented in infrastructure/core/__init__.py.

findings.summary: infrastructure/llm/__init__.py re-exports 60+ symbols already re-exported by review/__init__.py and core/__init__.py, creating a three-level barrel with no added policy
evidence: infrastructure/llm/__init__.py imports from infrastructure.llm.review (which itself re-exports from review.generator, review.io, review.metrics) then re-declares all 25+ review symbols in its own __all__
Callers importing from infrastructure.llm get the same symbols available directly from infrastructure.llm.review — the top-level barrel adds no abstraction or policy, only indirection
infrastructure/llm/__init__.py is 209 lines, almost entirely flat symbol forwarding
findings.suggestion: Trim infrastructure/llm/__init__.py to expose only the 5-8 symbols that are genuinely used across multiple callers via the package surface (LLMClient, LLMConfig, get_template, OutputValidator). Direct callers to submodule paths (infrastructure.llm.review, infrastructure.llm.core) for the rest. This removes ~150 lines of symbol forwarding with no functional loss.

findings.summary: infrastructure/scientific/validation.py uses 12+ repeated `# type: ignore` comments on dict list-appends, masking a fixable typing issue with defensive boilerplate
evidence: Lines 46, 48, 57, 58, 68, 69, 78, 80, 91, 92, 103, 105: every `validation_results[key].append(...)` and `validation_results[key] +=` carries a trailing `# type: ignore`
The root cause is that `validation_results` is typed as `Dict[str, Any]` but the suppressions are applied per-call rather than addressing the dict type once
Pattern is consistent with AI-generated code that suppresses type errors inline rather than correcting the declaration
findings.suggestion: Define a TypedDict (e.g. `ValidationResults`) with precise field types for `passed_tests`, `failed_tests`, `details`, `accuracy_score`. Replace the Dict[str, Any] declaration and remove all 12+ `# type: ignore` comments. Alternatively, assign the dict to a typed local variable once and let mypy infer the rest.

findings.summary: format_error_with_suggestions in logging_helpers.py has a 25-line docstring with embedded terminal output example for a 20-line function that simply formats an error object
evidence: Lines 10-41: the docstring includes a full multi-line example reproducing the exact output format already visible from reading the 5 if-branches below
The example adds no insight beyond what the code itself states — it restates the format rather than explaining the why or non-obvious constraints
The function body is 20 lines; the docstring is 30 lines
findings.suggestion: Reduce the docstring to a one-line summary plus Args/Returns sections. Move any non-obvious constraint (e.g. 'returns plain str(error) for non-TemplateError') into a short note. Remove the embedded terminal output reproduction.

findings.summary: validate_scientific_implementation repeats a 3-branch passed/failed/exception block with identical dict-append logic, violating DRY at the function body level
evidence: Lines 37-100: three structurally identical blocks each append a dict with keys test_index/input/expected/actual/status and increment passed_tests or failed_tests; only the status string and counter differ
The exception branch (lines 90-100) is a near-verbatim copy of the failed branch, adding only the `f'Exception: {e}'` substitution for actual
findings.suggestion: Extract a helper `_record_result(results, i, test_input, expected, actual, status)` that appends the dict and updates counters. Reduce the three identical blocks to three calls. This also makes adding new statuses (e.g. SKIP) a one-line change.
